summary: High‑quality soundboard captures a strong early‑Welnick era show with soaring Jerry, tight set and standout jams on Terrapin and Eyes of the World.
review: The recording is a clean SBD mix that delivers excellent frequency balance, though the keyboards sit a bit high in the second set. Musically the band is firing on all cylinders – Jerry’s lead work soars, Vince Welnick slots in unobtrusively and the first‑set classics flow into an experimental second set highlighted by a monster Terrapin jam and a 17‑minute, uniquely vibrant Eyes of the World.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers gave 4‑star ratings (one 5) and praised both performance and soundboard quality; consistent positive language across >8 detailed reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced SBD with great frequency representation. Minor issues: keyboards mixed a bit high in the second set and an occasional PA dropout (not audible on this SBD).

show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight first set of classic standards builds momentum, ending with a strong Deal. The second set ventures into experimental territory – high‑energy jams on Terrapin and Eyes of the World offset by a rougher Stella performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring lead guitar work and stepping up to cover for the new keyboardist; vocals described as youthful and inspiring.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince Welnick noted as unobtrusive and fitting in beautifully, though the second‑set keyboard mix is a bit high on this SBD.
